react.note: Bun auto-installs peer deps; resolved entry appears in lockfile packages section alongside runtime deps.

fsevents.note: optionalDependencies are main-group but must be flagged optional: true. fsevents is also darwin-only (os: darwin in lockfile).

mend_expected_behavior.trusted_dependencies_note: trustedDependencies is an install-script allowlist only. It has no effect on dep-tree shape. 'esbuild' is listed but is NOT a direct dep — Mend must not invent a dep entry for it.
known_failure_modes: Mend flattens all groups to 'main' — devDependencies loses group='dev' label.
Mend drops peerDependencies entirely because they are not in dependencies/devDependencies.
Mend drops fsevents because optional: true deps are excluded from the scan.
Mend misreads trustedDependencies as a dependency list and emits a ghost 'esbuild' dep.
Mend crashes on JSONC trailing commas in bun.lock before reaching any package entry.

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Mend will attempt npm-resolver fallback against bun.lock. JSONC comments and trailing commas may cause parse failures depending on Mend UA version.

peerDependencies (react) appear in the lockfile 'packages' section alongside runtime deps because Bun auto-installs peers. Whether Mend classifies them as group='main' or drops them is the probe's primary classification assertion.

optionalDependencies (fsevents) carry os='darwin' in the lockfile metadata. On a Linux scan host Bun would skip installation; Mend's static parser should still report the dep with optional: true regardless of the scan host OS.

trustedDependencies lists 'esbuild' which has no corresponding package in this manifest or lockfile. Mend must ignore unknown trust entries — if a ghost 'esbuild' dep appears in the output tree, that is a false positive.
typescript group='dev' is the key assertion for the dep-types-basic pattern. If Mend emits group='main' for typescript, the group classification code path is broken.
